title: 2 University of South Alabama professors pictured in controversial costumes are reinstated
body: Students, decent faculty, and the community should be much more vocal against what's happening here.  How can "all" students expect to receive the same treatment and consideration from a professor with the mindset that thinks this was okay.  Shame on you University of South Alabama.  And shame on us Alabamians who just allow this to continue to happen in our state and just decide to not be vocal and allow it to just go away.  Hence the very few comments here.
author: @jljones3461

title: Alabama - Dixieland Delight (Official Video)
body: This black girl loves Alabama, and I don't see racism, I only see Southern pride. I believe some use the music as a symbol of hate but it's always been the rich pitting the white poor against the poor blacks, and brown. I feel the same pride that any white man feels when I listen to Alabama. It has nothing to do with race to me just pride in time and a way of life and most importantly the struggle.
author: @EvenderDowns

title: Alabama - Dixieland Delight (Official Video)
body: 40+ years later my husband and I are still rollin' down a backwoods, Tennessee byway, one arm on the wheel. Life is good. Thank You Jesus.

title: Alabama - Dixieland Delight (Official Video)
body: 0:55 We dated for 2 years then got engaged. 6 months later we married. Our first date was on April 5th 1981. Our only child was born on April 5th 1985. We have been married for 43 years this October. We've been through some very rough times but the Lord Jesus is good and kept us strong. Praise the name of the Lord 🙌♥️🕊️
author: @dubyredburndarling6334

title: Alabama - Dixieland Delight (Official Video)
body: 57 year old here actually crying to this song . Only because I know we can never go back to this time and music
author: @mike3813

title: Alabama - Dixieland Delight (Official Video)
body: Growing up in Michigan, we loved Alabama's music. Their blue collar roots and rural America centered lyrics spoke to an entire generation all across the country.  I'm thankful for YouTube to be able to re-vist these moments in time. I can still recite so many of their songs lyrics even 40 years later. Sad to read of Jeff Cooks passing. I hope he and the rest of the band recognized the profound impact they had on so many.
author: @JamesReif

title: Alabama - Dixieland Delight (Official Video)
body: It absolutely gets no better than this....ever.  I'm 53 and wish more than ever I could go back to this time again.
author: @rogerw.3935

title: Alabama - Dixieland Delight (Official Video)
body: This is how the country looked when I was growing up. The cars and trucks, how they're dressed, that back road with the sun setting behind the trees, the old homes especially the one with all the chairs on the front porch and a clothes line out back, the tractor, the old store, even the saw mill. I watch this video often just to go back to a time when the world was a little kinder and sane.
author: @angelamcclure7905

title: Alabama - Dixieland Delight (Official Video)
body: As much as I love this song, can we take a moment to appreciate that cool old Chevy pickup? 4-doors weren't too common back then!

In her Amazon storefront you can see some of the things that she used to decorate her room and they do not cost an insane amount of money. title: Exploring Wealthy Alabama 🇺🇸
body: I just want to take a moment  to thank @PeterSantenello for taking time to visit the Fairhope Alabama area. Like us all, it’s easy to talk about where we are from, but Peter takes it as personal as we would like, and it is appreciated. Thank all of you for taking time out of your day to explore a place I’m obviously fond of. All of you rock!!!
author: @douglaskilborn151

title: Exploring Wealthy Alabama 🇺🇸
body: I live in Europe, Norway. Youre videos are nothing like the stuff we read in the newspaper or watch on the news. This is the honest truth, thank you Peter for showing us the real America outside the media. Thank you, cheers
author: @JohnSorlie

title: Exploring Wealthy Alabama 🇺🇸
body: Raised in Lake Tahoe California I was stationed at Fort Mcclean Alabama. I remember my first trip into the local town off base I was a bit freaked out. People would come up out of the blue and just start a conversation.  I got invited to 2 different family's homes for dinner.  Yes, I was in Uniform, but I have never been treated so nice by people ever in my life.  Also, I stopped at a rundown looking breakfast place a mom-and-POP business.  Best breakfast i have ever had. The wife kept coming out adding more hash browns and gravy saying I was a growing boy and needed a full breakfast.
author: @RickLancaster-w2x

title: Exploring Wealthy Alabama 🇺🇸
body:
True story..
My parents bought a lake house in northeastern Alabama. On the first day they stayed there a close neighbor down the street came over and introduced himself and told my parents if they ever needed a ride that they could take his. Car doors are always unlocked and keys are in the ignition. 😂. Also, neighbors would bring us fresh fruit , fish, etc. neighbors would randomly mow our lawn on occasions if we haven't been to the property in awhile. Another neighbor had a key to the house to check up on it when we were gone, etc.
author: @SC68170

title: UNIVERSITY OF ALABAMA *CAMPUS TOUR* | Classrooms, Sorority Row, Stadium, Starbucks, Library, Dorms
body:
Many things changed, since I was a student at UoA (1977-1984), the ΔΔΔ house was across the street from where it is now and it was round.
Barnwell field, that we used to play soccer is now a parking lot.
But the ΔΓ anchor, even with it's new brass color (it was dark colored in my days) reminded me of a time that our ΘΤ (back then it was a men's fraternity and ΔΓs were our Little Sisters, I think this changed now because in the new form ΘΤ became coed because is was a professional engineering fraternity and I believe the old scheme was dissolved for a while) pledges hand cuffed me on the anchor and as our members were chasing them around, in order to get the handcuff's key to free me, they lost the key in the grass lol.
I got so mad with the whole thing, that I put the double side of the cuff my hand was tied with, over a blade near the top of the ΔΓ anchor and using my arm as a lever, I broke the pin of the hand cuffs and set my self free, with only minor cuts on the sides of my wrist.
So the ΔΓ anchor has it's history with me too lol.
author: @FLORATOSOTHON

title: Ultimate Boston Campus Tour #boston #mit #bostoncollege #bu
body: Why should we take you opinion on any of your reviews. What are your qualifications? Who asked you to do this? What’s your major? How long have you been attending the academic institution you’ve been enrolled in? Are you a professional student? So just because you know these institutions of higher learning doesn’t mean you know the bings that are important to me specifically. Thanks, but no thanks!
author: @stephenino

title: Welcome Message & Campus Tour
body:
Wow! It is totally unrecognizable! I started way back in Fall 1984 and the last time I was on campus was for graduation in 1988. A few years ago I tried to make a virtual visit using Google Earth and I could barely recognize a few things! Back then our EE building was like a poor man's garage in comparison to this! Obviously it was much cheaper then :) I have great memories of the old campus and if I could turn time back, I would do things so differently.
What I miss is that Alumni relations is totally unsuitable for alumni that is abroad that would never benefit from much of what the current Alumni Association offers. I feel, all of us who are honored to have studied there, are out of the loop. I would certainly like to get in touch with old pals from there (that doesn´t seem possible either).
I recognized Beta dorms in the video, is Delta and Alpha still there? it would have been nice to have seen them featured too. I still remember my Student ID and my USA P.O. Box. (is there still an on-campus postal address?). Used to write for the USA Vanguard as well, great memories.
author: @PanamaVibes

title: Welcome Message & Campus Tour
body: I'd like to know why there is past history hidden from us all. Oh....my direct ancestor is one of the reasons why we all have a U.S. 1st Amendment. I surely can explain.
author: @brianparent
